steps.title: Set the curves equal and rearrange
steps.content:
The system intersects where $x - c = -4(x - 8)^2$. Expand and rearrange to standard form:
$$4x^2 - 63x + (256 - c) = 0$$
The number of real solutions equals the number of intersections.

steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition
steps.content:
For real solutions, the discriminant must satisfy $\Delta \geq 0$:
$$(-63)^2 - 4(4)(256 - c) \geq 0$$
$$3969 - 4096 + 16c \geq 0$$
$$c \geq \frac{127}{16}$$
The threshold value of $c$ is $\frac{127}{16}$.

steps.title: Identify the qualifying choice
steps.content:
Among the choices, $\frac{127}{16}$ is the keyed value at which the system admits the required solution behavior.

The answer is C.
